pattern: Binary searching the answer length and testing each candidate with rolling hashes shared across all paths finds the longest contiguous run common to every path.
hints: If a common subpath of length L exists, so does one of every smaller length, which makes the answer monotonic and binary-searchable.
To test a length L, collect the multiset of length-L window fingerprints for each path and intersect them across all paths.
Use a rolling polynomial hash so each path's windows are computed in linear time; double hashing guards against collisions.

edge_inputs.description: anchor example common run of length two
anchor example reversed path shares single cities
long shared prefix but interrupted later

stress.description: several paths built around a planted common segment over a small city set
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, 8)
    L = rng.randint(1, 5)
    common = []
    last = -1
    for _ in range(L):
        v = rng.randrange(n)
        while v == last:
            v = rng.randrange(n)
        common.append(v)
        last = v
    m = rng.randint(2, 4)
    paths = []
    for _ in range(m):
        pre = []
        last2 = -1
        for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 4)):
            v = rng.randrange(n)
            while v == last2:
                v = rng.randrange(n)
            pre.append(v)
            last2 = v
        # ensure junction with common does not create consecutive dup
        if pre and common and pre[-1] == common[0]:
            pre.pop()
        suf = []
        last3 = common[-1] if common else -1
        for _ in range(rng.randint(0, 4)):
            v = rng.randrange(n)
            while v == last3:
                v = rng.randrange(n)
            suf.append(v)
            last3 = v
        path = pre + common + suf
        # final pass to remove any accidental consecutive duplicates
        clean = []
        for v in path:
            if not clean or clean[-1] != v:
                clean.append(v)
        if not clean:
            clean = [rng.randrange(n)]
        paths.append(clean)
    return [n, paths]
